page: Microsoft Azure F80ads_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the Fadsv7 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. It features 80 dedicated physical cores and 80 vCPUs with a single thread per core, operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The processor configuration includes a cache hierarchy of 48 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 320.0 GB of system memory, providing a ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance includes 9449 GB of local disk capacity and is Premium Storage capable. It runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

In benchmark evaluations, the F80ads_v7 demonstrates top-tier performance across multiple compute and memory metrics. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests place this instance in the top 10% of tested systems, with scores of 3767.09 and 290168.74 respectively. Memory bandwidth is also highly rated, reaching 14260.32 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 476.02 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Database operations and key-value store performance are notable, with the PassMark database operations test scoring in the top tier and Redis SET operations achieving 202.34 million operations per second.

For language model workloads, the instance shows varying performance tiers depending on the model scale. Prompt processing speeds are strong to top-tier, reaching 5722.82 tokens per second for a small 135M model and 294.76 tokens per second for a medium 7B model. However, text generation speeds present a tradeoff, dropping to an average tier of 231.82 tokens per second for the 135M model, though text generation for the 7B model remains in the top tier at 54.82 tokens per second. The lack of a dedicated hardware GPU accelerator limits its utility for heavy deep learning training workloads, positioning it instead as a CPU-driven inference and high-throughput data processing platform.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the F80ads_v7 offers a dense allocation of dedicated physical cores and a substantial 9449 GB local storage volume, balancing high-frequency compute with local storage capacity. This profile makes the instance suitable for database hosting, large-scale web serving, data decompression pipelines, and memory-intensive general-purpose workloads that do not require GPU acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure F80ads_v7 is an x86_64 server instance from the Fadsv7 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. It features 80 dedicated physical cores, 80 vCPUs, 320.0 GB of RAM, and 9449 GB of local storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the instance achieves top-tier benchmark results, including high-ranking stress-ng CPU scores and a Redis throughput of 202.34 million operations per second. Large-block read memory bandwidth reaches 476.02 GB/sec. While prompt processing for language models is strong, text generation speeds present a relative performance tradeoff. Lacking a GPU, this instance is structured for high-density compute workloads, database hosting, and data decompression pipelines where massive local storage and high-frequency single-threaded performance are required.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F80ads_v7 is an x86_64 server featuring 80 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 320 GB RAM, and 9449 GB local storage, delivering top-tier database and multi-core CPU benchmark performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F80ads_v7 is an x86_64 server with 80 AMD EPYC cores, 320 GB RAM, and 9449 GB local storage, optimized for high-throughput workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency eighty-core processor instance with massive local storage and top-tier memory bandwidth for intensive database and data processing workloads.
bullet_points: 80 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 physical cores operating at 4.5 GHz
320.0 GB of system memory paired with 9449 GB of local storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Redis throughput reaching 202.34 million operations per second
Suited for database hosting, web serving, and data decompression pipelines
